shots.visual_caption: Close-up of a young man standing on stage with hands clasped, shifting from a questioning expression in the first frame to a surprised look in the last, no camera motion.
Close-up of a young man in a black Stanford-branded shirt speaking passionately, transitioning from "STANFORD" to "STUDENT" in bold text while keeping the same framing.
Static close-up of a young man standing on stage with text transitioning from "STUDENT" to "THINKS," no motion.
Young man speaking on stage with an expressive gesture, camera zooming in on his face as the text changes from "HE'S" to "STEVE JOBS."
Static medium shot of a young man in a black jacket and jeans speaking on stage with the text "STEVE JOBS" overlayed, no motion.
Close-up of a young man standing on stage, slightly changing his expression from neutral to smiling, no motion aside from facial change.
Close-up of a young man speaking with his hands clasped, transitioning from a wide-open mouth expression to a more closed mouth, with slight rotation of the head.
Close-up of a young man speaking directly to the camera, with the text changing from "THIS" to "TONY" across his forehead, no motion.
Close-up of a young man with a headband speaking directly to the camera, shifting his expression between frames as he continues his thought.
Close-up of a young man mock boxing, transitioning from a poised stance to a full punch extension, with text overlays changing from "HE'S" to "WALNUT."
Static close-up of a young man with a headband boxing stance, captioned "WALNUT," no motion.
Close-up of a young man playfully mimicking boxing movements while holding a walnut, with a slight change in expression from animated to focused.

shots.visual_caption: Static split-screen with a young man speaking on stage about "Rhythm is everywhere" while a surprised individual below reacts, no motion.
shots.spoken_window: Do you think Tony will be the next Steve Jobs

hook_speech: Who's the Stanford student that thinks he's Steve Jobs This is Tony and he's building

sfx_context.pattern_summary: The sound effects are primarily used to emphasize key moments in the dialogue, with a higher frequency during the hook and a consistent application of tonal effects. The cadence is frequent, with sound effects occurring on important claims and questions.

rules.example: Do you think Tony will be the next Steve Jobs
